member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OrientationFlipInformation
member.summary: Specifies the orientation (front/back) configuration of the robot arm. Determined by the position of the B-axis rotation center relative to the S-axis.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OrientationFlipInformation.Front
member.summary: Front configuration - B-axis rotation center is in front of S-axis rotation center when viewed from the right-hand side of the robot.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OrientationFlipInformation.Back
member.summary: Back configuration - B-axis rotation center is behind S-axis rotation center when viewed from the right-hand side of the robot.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ArmFlipInformation
member.summary: Specifies the arm configuration (upper/lower) based on L and U axis positions.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ArmFlipInformation.Upper
member.summary: Upper arm configuration - elbow is above the line between shoulder and wrist.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ArmFlipInformation.Lower
member.summary: Lower arm configuration - elbow is below the line between shoulder and wrist.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.AxisFlipInformation
member.summary: Specifies whether an axis angle is less than or greater than/equal to 180 degrees. Used for determining robot configuration in multi-solution situations.

member.summary: Axis angle is less than 180 degrees.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.AxisFlipInformation.UT180
member.summary: Axis angle is greater than or equal to 180 degrees.

member.summary: Specifies how to handle position in reverse direction scenarios.

member.summary: Use the previous position as reference.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RegardedReversePositionSpecified.Form
member.summary: Use the specified form/posture as reference.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.FlipNoFlipInformation
member.summary: Specifies the flip/no-flip wrist configuration.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.FlipNoFlipInformation.Flip
member.summary: Flip configuration - wrist is in flipped orientation.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.FlipNoFlipInformation.NoFlip
member.summary: No-flip configuration - wrist is in standard orientation.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPosture
member.summary: Represents the complete robot posture (form) configuration. Encodes the kinematic configuration choices that determine which of multiple inverse kinematics solutions is used to reach a Cartesian position.
member.remarks: For a 6-axis robot, there can be up to 8 different joint configurations that achieve the same tool position and orientation. The posture data specifies which configuration to use.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPosture.Default
member.summary: Default posture with Form=0 and ExtendedForm=0.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPosture.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new RobotPosture with default values.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPosture.Form
member.summary: Gets or sets the primary form byte encoding basic posture flags. Bit-encoded: orientation, arm, flip, R-axis, T-axis, S-axis, redundant, reverse position.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPosture.ExtendedForm
member.summary: Gets or sets the extended form byte for additional axis configurations. Bit-encoded: L-axis, U-axis, B-axis, E-axis, W-axis range flags.

member.summary: Creates a new RobotPosture with specified form values.

L-axis angle range configuration.
U-axis angle range configuration.
B-axis angle range configuration.
E-axis angle range configuration.
W-axis angle range configuration.

member.summary: Converts the posture to a single 16-bit integer value. Form is stored in the low byte, ExtendedForm in the high byte.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.InvalidDataAnswerException
member.summary: Exception thrown when the robot controller returns an error response to a High Speed Ethernet Server command. This exception contains detailed status codes that help identify the specific error condition.
member.remarks: The High Speed Ethernet Server protocol uses status codes to indicate various error conditions such as invalid commands, permission errors, hardware faults, or communication issues. The Status property contains the primary error code, while AddedStatus provides additional context.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.InvalidDataAnswerException.Status
member.summary: Gets the primary status code returned by the robot controller. A value of 0 indicates success; any other value indicates an error condition.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.InvalidDataAnswerException.AddedStatus
member.summary: Gets the additional status code providing more detailed error information. The interpretation of this value depends on the primary Status code.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader
member.summary: Specialized binary reader for parsing Yaskawa High Speed Ethernet Server UDP response packets. Handles the protocol header parsing and provides safe read methods that check for end-of-stream. The packet format follows the Yaskawa High Speed Ethernet Server communication protocol specification.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader._stream
member.summary: Internal memory stream holding the raw packet data received from the robot controller.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.#ctor(System.IO.MemoryStream,System.Net.IPEndPoint)
member.summary: Creates a new PacketReader and parses the response packet header. The constructor validates the packet format and extracts header information.
member.param: Memory stream containing the raw UDP packet data.
IP endpoint of the robot controller that sent the response.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Releases the resources used by the PacketReader and underlying stream.
member.param: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.HasBytesRemaining(System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks whether the stream has reached the end or has insufficient bytes remaining.
member.param: Number of bytes required for the next read operation.
member.returns: True if there are enough bytes remaining to read; false otherwise.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.ReadInt32Safe
member.summary: Reads a 4-byte signed integer from the stream, returning default value if at end of stream.
member.returns: The 32-bit signed integer read from the stream, or 0 if stream ended.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.ReadInt16Safe
member.summary: Reads a 2-byte signed integer from the stream, returning default value if at end of stream.
member.returns: The 16-bit signed integer read from the stream, or 0 if stream ended.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.ReadByteSafe
member.summary: Reads a single byte from the stream, returning default value if at end of stream.
member.returns: The byte read from the stream, or 0 if stream ended.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.ReadSingleSafe
member.summary: Reads a 4-byte floating point number from the stream, returning default value if at end of stream.
member.returns: The single-precision floating point number read from the stream, or 0 if stream ended.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.ReadBytesSafe(System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads the specified number of bytes from the stream, returning empty array if insufficient data.
member.param: Number of bytes to read.
member.returns: A byte array containing the data read, or empty array if stream ended.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader._99999999_BYTES
member.summary: Reserved field bytes representing "99999999" in ASCII - used in the packet header. This is part of the Yaskawa protocol specification for request identification.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader._YERC_BYTES
member.summary: Protocol identifier bytes "YERC " (with space and null) - marks the start of every packet. This magic number identifies the packet as a Yaskawa Ethernet Robot Controller message.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader._PADDING_2_BYTES
member.summary: Two-byte padding used for alignment in the packet structure.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.Identifier
member.summary: Packet identifier counter - currently fixed at 0. Note: This could be incremented for tracking request-response pairs in concurrent scenarios.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.DataHeader
member.summary: Contains metadata about the response packet including IP endpoint, data size, and block information.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader.Send(System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient,System.Int16,System.Int16,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sends a command packet to the robot controller and optionally waits for a response. This method constructs a properly formatted UDP packet following the High Speed Ethernet Server protocol.
member.param: UDP client connected to the robot controller.
Command number identifying the operation to perform (e.g., 0x75 for position reading).
Instance identifier for the command, typically specifies control group or index.
Attribute byte for the command, context-dependent on command type.
Service type: 0x01 for read, 0x02 for write, 0x10 for command execution, etc.
Optional payload data to send with the command.
Block number for multi-block transfers, used for file operations.
True if this is the final block in a multi-block transfer.
True to wait for and return a response; false for fire-and-forget commands.
True for file control operations (uses processing division 2).
True to set the ACK flag in the request header.
member.returns: PacketReader containing the parsed response, or null if hasAnswer is false.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandType
member.summary: Specifies the type of position command (motion instruction) to execute. Determines the path type and whether position is absolute or incremental.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandType.LinkAbsolute
member.summary: Link (joint interpolated) motion to an absolute position. All joints move simultaneously to reach the target, resulting in non-linear TCP path.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandType.StraightAbsolute
member.summary: Straight (linear interpolated) motion to an absolute position. TCP moves in a straight line to the target position.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandType.StraightIncrement
member.summary: Straight (linear interpolated) motion by an incremental offset. TCP moves in a straight line relative to current position.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandClassification
member.summary: Specifies the speed classification (units) for motion commands. Determines how the speed value is interpreted by the controller.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandClassification.LinkPercent
member.summary: Speed as percentage of maximum link (joint) speed. Valid range: 0.01 to 100.00 percent.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandClassification.Cartesian_MM_S
member.summary: Speed in millimeters per second for linear motion. Valid range depends on robot model.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandClassification.Cartesian_DEG_S
member.summary: Speed in degrees per second for rotational motion. Valid range depends on robot model.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandOperationCoordinate
member.summary: Specifies the coordinate system for position command interpretation. Determines how X, Y, Z, Rx, Ry, Rz values are interpreted.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandOperationCoordinate.Base
member.summary: Base coordinate system (world frame at robot base). Fixed reference frame typically aligned with robot mounting.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandOperationCoordinate.Robot
member.summary: Robot coordinate system. Reference frame at the robot's origin point.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandOperationCoordinate.User
member.summary: User-defined coordinate system. Custom reference frame defined for specific workpiece or fixture.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PositionCommandOperationCoordinate.Tool
member.summary: Tool coordinate system. Reference frame at the tool center point (TCP).

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotRecentAlarm
member.summary: Specifies which recent alarm to retrieve from the robot controller. The controller maintains a history of the most recent alarms.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotRecentAlarm.Latest
member.summary: The most recent (current) alarm.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotRecentAlarm.SecondLatest
member.summary: The second most recent alarm.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotRecentAlarm.ThirdLatest
member.summary: The third most recent alarm.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotRecentAlarm.FourthLatest
member.summary: The fourth most recent alarm.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.AlarmResetType
member.summary: Specifies the type of alarm reset operation to perform.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.AlarmResetType.Reset
member.summary: Reset the current alarm. Requires the alarm condition to be resolved.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.AlarmResetType.Cancel
member.summary: Cancel the current error. Used for recoverable errors.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData
member.summary: Contains information about a robot alarm retrieved from the controller. Alarms indicate error conditions that may require operator intervention.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData.Code
member.summary: Gets the alarm code identifying the specific alarm type. Alarm codes are documented in the robot's maintenance manual.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData.Data
member.summary: Gets additional alarm data providing context about the alarm. The meaning depends on the specific alarm code.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData.Type
member.summary: Gets the alarm type/category classification.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData.OccurringTime
member.summary: Gets the timestamp when the alarm occurred. Format: "YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS" (16 characters).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData.Text
member.summary: Gets the alarm message text describing the alarm condition. Maximum 32 characters.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotAlarmData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmDataExtended
member.summary: Contains extended information about a robot alarm, including sub-code details. Provides more detailed diagnostic information than the basic RobotAlarmData.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmDataExtended.AdditionnalInformation
member.summary: Gets additional information about the alarm condition. Maximum 16 characters.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmDataExtended.SubData
member.summary: Gets the sub-code data providing detailed error context. Maximum 96 characters.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmDataExtended.SubDataReverse
member.summary: Gets the reversed sub-code data. Maximum 96 characters.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotAlarmDataExtended.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotAlarmDataExtended with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotData
member.summary: Base class for all robot data response objects returned by High Speed Ethernet Server commands. Contains common header information about the communication response.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotData.Header
member.summary: Gets or sets the response header containing metadata about the communication. Hidden from property browsers as it's primarily for internal use.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotData.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a blank instance of RobotData, for use as input to robot commands (e.g. kinematics conversions).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing IP endpoint, data size, and block information.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader
member.summary: Contains header information extracted from a High Speed Ethernet Server response packet. Provides metadata about the communication including source address, data size, and transfer status.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader.IP
member.summary: Gets the IP endpoint (address and port) of the robot controller that sent the response. Useful for identifying the source in multi-robot configurations.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader.DataSize
member.summary: Gets the size of the data payload in the response packet. Does not include the header size.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader.BlockNo
member.summary: Gets the block number for multi-block transfers. The most significant bit (0x80000000) indicates this is the last block. Used primarily for file transfer operations.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader.IsLastBlock
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this is the last block in a multi-block transfer. Returns true when the MSB of BlockNo is set (0x80000000).

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData
member.summary: Contains the content of a file downloaded from the robot controller. Provides methods for parsing structured file content such as job files and parameter files.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData.Headers
member.summary: Gets the list of response headers from multi-block transfers. Large files are transferred in multiple UDP packets.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotFileContentData with no header.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.List{UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader})
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotFileContentData with the specified headers.
member.param: List of response headers from the file download operation.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData.Content
member.summary: Gets the text content of the downloaded file.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData.ContentRaw
member.summary: Gets the text content of the downloaded file.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileContentData.FileName
member.summary: Gets the name of the downloaded file.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileListData
member.summary: Contains the result of a file listing operation on the robot controller. Returns an array of file names matching the specified pattern.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileListData.Headers
member.summary: Gets the list of response headers from multi-block transfers. File listings may span multiple UDP packets for large directories.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileListData.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.List{UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader})
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotFileListData with the specified headers.
member.param: List of response headers from the file list operation.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotFileListData.Files
member.summary: Gets the array of file names returned by the listing operation. File names include extensions (e.g., "MYJOB.JBI", "SYSTEM.SYS").

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotIOData
member.summary: Represents data returned from reading multiple I/O (Input/Output) points from the robot controller. I/O addresses are organized in groups based on their function and accessibility.
member.remarks:
Valid I/O address ranges:
1-512: Robot user input signals
1001-1512: Robot user output signals
2001-2512: External input signals
2701-2956: Network input signals
3001-3512: External output signals
3701-3956: Network output signals
4001-4160: Robot system input signals
5001-5300: Robot system output signals
6001-6064: Interface panel input signals
7001-7999: Auxiliary relay signals
8001-8128: Robot control status signals

member.summary: Represents data returned from reading multiple position variables (P variables) from the robot controller. P variables store complete robot position data including coordinates, posture, tool and user coordinate references.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobData
member.summary: Contains information about the currently executing job (program) on the robot controller. Retrieved using the executing job information reading command.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobData.Name
member.summary: Gets the name of the currently selected/executing job. Job names can be up to 32 characters. Returns empty string if no job is selected.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobData.Line
member.summary: Gets the current line number being executed within the job. Line numbers are 1-based and correspond to the job listing.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobData.Step
member.summary: Gets the current step number within the job. Step numbers track the execution progress through motion instructions.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobData.SpeedOverride
member.summary: Gets the current speed override percentage (0-100). This is the global speed multiplier applied to all motions.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotJobData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobStackData
member.summary: Contains the job call stack for a specific task on the robot controller. Represents the current nesting of CALL instructions, from the outermost job to the currently executing one. Only supported on DX200 (AY/BY/YN) controllers.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobStackData.Jobs
member.summary: Gets the job names in the call stack, outermost first. The first entry is the root job, the last entry is the currently executing job. Empty if no nested calls are active.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotJobStackData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotJobStackData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.KinematicsConversionMode.JointToCartesian
member.summary: Forward kinematics: convert joint angles to a Cartesian position (YERC command 0x0362). Input DataType should be 0 (pulse/joint), axes in 0.0001° units. Output is Cartesian (µm for XYZ, 0.0001° for orientation axes).

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData
member.summary: Base class for kinematic position data exchanged with the robot controller. Contains coordinate type, posture flags, tool/user numbers, and the 8 raw axis values.
member.remarks:
Axis value scaling:
Joint positions: values in 0.0001° units (divide by 10 000 for degrees).
Cartesian positions: XYZ axes (0–2) in µm; orientation axes (3–5) in 0.0001°.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData.DataType
member.summary: Gets or sets the coordinate type of this position.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData.Form
member.summary: Gets or sets the posture/form flags for this position.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData.ToolNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets the tool number used for this position.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData.UserCoordinateNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets the user coordinate number used for this position.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData.Axes
member.summary: Gets or sets the 8 raw axis values. For joint positions: values in 0.0001° units (divide by 10 000 for degrees). For Cartesian positions: XYZ axes (0–2) in µm; orientation axes (3–5) in 0.0001°.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsPositionData.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a blank instance for use as input to kinematics conversion methods.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData
member.summary: Cartesian position result from a kinematics conversion. Provides named X/Y/Z and orientation properties in engineering units in addition to the raw axis values.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData.X
member.summary: Gets the X position in millimetres (derived from the raw µm axis value).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData.Y
member.summary: Gets the Y position in millimetres.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData.Z
member.summary: Gets the Z position in millimetres.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData.Rx
member.summary: Gets the Rx orientation in degrees (derived from the raw 0.0001° axis value).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData.Ry
member.summary: Gets the Ry orientation in degrees.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsCartesianData.Rz
member.summary: Gets the Rz orientation in degrees.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsJointData
member.summary: Joint-space position result from a kinematics conversion. Provides the 8 joint axis values in both raw 0.0001° units and as a ready-to-use degrees array.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotKinematicsJointData.AxisDegrees
member.summary: Gets the 8 joint axis values converted to degrees. Each element equals the corresponding value divided by 10 000.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotManagementTimeData
member.summary: Contains management time information for tracking robot operation statistics. Provides uptime and usage metrics for maintenance planning and reporting.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotManagementTimeData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotManagementTimeData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotManagementTimeData.StartTime
member.summary: Gets the start time of the tracked period. Format: "YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM" (16 characters).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotManagementTimeData.EllapseTime
member.summary: Gets the elapsed time for the tracked metric. Format: "HHHH:MM:SS.ss" or similar time duration format (12 characters).

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType
member.summary: Specifies the type of management time data to retrieve. Different metrics track various aspects of robot operation.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.ControlPowerOnTime
member.summary: Total time the controller power has been on.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.ServoPowerOnTimeTotal
member.summary: Total servo power on time across all robots.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.ServoPowerOnTimR1ToR8
member.summary: Servo power on time for robots R1 through R8. Add robot number (0-7) to get specific robot.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.ServoPowerOnTimeS1ToS24
member.summary: Servo power on time for stations S1 through S24. Add station number (0-23) to get specific station.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.PlayBackTimeTotal
member.summary: Total playback time across all robots.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.PlayBackTimeR1ToR8
member.summary: Playback time for robots R1 through R8. Add robot number (0-7) to get specific robot.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.PlayBackTimeS1ToS24
member.summary: Playback time for stations S1 through S24. Add station number (0-23) to get specific station.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.MotionTimeTotal
member.summary: Total motion time across all robots.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.MotionTimeR1ToR8
member.summary: Motion time for robots R1 through R8. Add robot number (0-7) to get specific robot.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.MotionTimeS1ToS24
member.summary: Motion time for stations S1 through S24. Add station number (0-23) to get specific station.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType.OperationTimeApplication1To8
member.summary: Operation time for applications 1 through 8. Add application number (0-7) to get specific application.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OnOffCommandType
member.summary: Specifies the type of ON/OFF command to send to the robot controller. These commands control fundamental robot states that affect safety and operation.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OnOffCommandType.Hold
member.summary: Hold command - pauses robot motion while maintaining servo power. Robot can resume from held position. Value: 1.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OnOffCommandType.Servo
member.summary: Servo power command - enables or disables motor power to the robot. Servo must be ON for the robot to move. Value: 2.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OnOffCommandType.HLock
member.summary: Lock Teach Pendant. Value: 3.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPluralData`1
member.summary: Represents a collection of data values returned from plural (batch) read operations. Used for reading multiple variables, registers, or I/O points in a single request.
member.typeparam: The type of values contained in the collection.
member.remarks: Plural read commands are more efficient than individual reads when accessing multiple consecutive data points, as they reduce network round-trips.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPluralData`1.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotPluralData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPluralData`1.Value
member.summary: Gets the array of values read from the robot controller. The array length corresponds to the number of values successfully read.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionData`1
member.summary: Represents generic robot position data with axis values of the specified type. This class provides a flexible structure for storing position information that can be represented in different data formats (pulse values, Cartesian coordinates, etc.).
member.typeparam: The type used to represent axis values (typically int for pulse values or double for coordinates).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionData`1.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotPositionData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionData`1.Form
member.summary: Gets or sets the robot posture (form) data defining the robot's kinematic configuration. This includes flip/no-flip state, arm configuration (upper/lower), and axis angle ranges.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionData`1.DataType
member.summary: Gets or sets the position data type indicating the coordinate system used. Determines how axis values should be interpreted (pulse, base, robot, user, or tool coordinates).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionData`1.ToolNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets the tool number (TCP - Tool Center Point) used for this position. Tool numbers typically range from 0-63, where 0 is often the robot flange center.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionData`1.UserCoordinateNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets the user coordinate system number used for this position. User coordinates define custom reference frames for specific workpiece locations.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionIntData
member.summary: Represents robot position data with 32-bit integer axis values. This is the primary type used for pulse-based position data from the High Speed Ethernet Server. Axis values are in pulse units (encoder counts) or scaled coordinate values.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotPositionIntData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotPositionIntData with the specified header information.
member.param: Response header containing metadata about the communication.

member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotPositionIntData from a generic RobotPositionData. Used for conversion from generic types to specific types.
member.param: Source position data to copy from.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotBasePositionType
member.summary: Defines the type of base position data representation.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotBasePositionType.PulseValue
member.summary: Position is represented as encoder pulse values.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotBasePositionType.BaseCoordinateValue
member.summary: Position is represented in base coordinate system values.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotBasePositionData
member.summary: Represents base position data for coordinated motion with travel units or external bases. Base positions define the location of the robot's base in world coordinates or pulse values.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotBasePositionData.DataType

member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotBasePositionData with the specified header information.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotExternalAxisData
member.summary: Represents external axis position data for positioners, travel units, or additional servo axes. External axes are coordinated with the robot motion for applications like welding positioners.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotExternalAxisData.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotDataHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of RobotExternalAxisData with the specified header information.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData
member.summary: Contains the current operational status of the robot controller. Provides information about the robot's mode, running state, and safety conditions. Retrieved using the status information reading command.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Step
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is in step (single-step) execution mode. When true, the robot executes one instruction at a time.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Cycle
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is in cycle execution mode. When true, the robot executes one complete cycle then stops.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Automatic
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is in automatic operation mode. When true, the robot can operate automatically without pendant interaction.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Running
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is currently running (executing a job).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.InGuardSafeOperation
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is in guard safe operation mode. Indicates collaborative/safety-rated operation mode is active.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Teach
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is in teach mode. In teach mode, the robot can be manually positioned and jobs can be edited.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Play
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is in play mode. In play mode, the robot can execute programmed jobs.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.CommandRemote
member.summary: Gets whether remote command mode is enabled. When true, the robot accepts commands from external sources (including this API).

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.InHoldStatusPendant
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is held by the programming pendant. Operator has pressed hold on the pendant.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.InHoldStatusExternally
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is held by an external hold signal. External safety circuit has triggered a hold condition.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.InHoldStatusByCommand
member.summary: Gets whether the robot is held by a command (software hold). A hold command was issued via the API or job instruction.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.Alarming
member.summary: Gets whether an alarm is currently active. Check GetAlarm() for detailed alarm information.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.ErrorOccurring
member.summary: Gets whether an error condition is occurring. Errors may prevent normal operation until resolved.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotStatusData.ServoOn
member.summary: Gets whether servo power is enabled. Servo must be ON for the robot to move.

member.summary: Cycle mode - robot executes one complete cycle of the program then stops. Useful for testing or single-part operations.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.SwitchingCommands.Continue

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerClient
member.summary: Main client class for communicating with Yaskawa Motoman industrial robots using the High Speed Ethernet Server protocol. This class provides methods for reading robot status, positions, variables, and controlling robot operations via UDP.

remarks.para: The High Speed Ethernet Server protocol operates over UDP and provides high-speed data exchange capabilities for real-time robot monitoring and control applications.
Before using this class, ensure the robot controller has the High Speed Ethernet Server function enabled and properly configured. Default ports are 10040 (data) and 10041 (file).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerClient.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new instance of HighSpeedEServerClient for robot communication. Call Connect() to establish communication with a robot controller.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerClient.Connect(System.String,UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters)
member.summary: Connects to a robot controller with custom connection parameters object.
member.param: IP address or hostname of the robot controller.
Connection parameters including ports and timeouts.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters
member.summary: Base class defining connection parameters for the High Speed Ethernet Server communication. This class cannot be instantiated directly; use a derived class or use Connect with optional parameters. Allows customization of timeouts and ports for different network environments.
member.remarks: Default values are suitable for most local network configurations. Increase timeouts for high-latency networks or when experiencing timeout errors.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DEFAULT_DATA_TIMEOUT_MILLISECONDS
member.summary: Default timeout in milliseconds for data commands (1500ms).

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DEFAULT_POWER_ON_TIMEOUT_MILLISECONDS
member.summary: Default timeout in milliseconds for servo power on operations (8000ms).

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DEFAULT_FILE_TIMEOUT_MILLISECONDS
member.summary: Default timeout in milliseconds for file operations (4000ms).

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DEFAULT_DATA_PORT
member.summary: Default UDP port for data communication (10040).

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DEFAULT_FILE_PORT
member.summary: Default UDP port for file transfer operations (10041).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the connection parameters class.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DataTimeoutMilliseconds
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a response to data commands. Applies to most read/write operations like position reading, variable access, etc. Default: 1500ms.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.PowerOnTimeoutMilliseconds
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum time in milliseconds to wait for servo power on to complete. Servo power on may take longer due to brake release and motor initialization. Default: 8000ms.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.FileTimeoutMilliseconds
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum time in milliseconds to wait for file operation responses. File operations may be slower due to larger data transfers and disk I/O on the controller. Default: 4000ms.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.DataPort
member.summary: Gets or sets the UDP port number for data communication. Must match the robot controller's High Speed Ethernet Server data port configuration. Default: 10040.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.HighSpeedEServerConnectParameters.FilePort
member.summary: Gets or sets the UDP port number for file transfer operations. Must match the robot controller's High Speed Ethernet Server file port configuration. Default: 10041.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase
member.summary: Base class implementing the High Speed Ethernet Server protocol for Yaskawa robot communication. Provides methods for reading robot status, positions, variables, and executing commands via UDP.

remarks.para: This class implements the Yaskawa High Speed Ethernet Server protocol which uses UDP packets for high-speed bidirectional communication with robot controllers.

The protocol supports various command categories:
Status and alarm reading (commands 0x70-0x77)
Variable reading and writing (commands 0x78-0x81)
Robot control commands (commands 0x82-0x8B)
Plural (batch) data operations (commands 0x300-0x309)
File operations (file control commands)

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase._client
member.summary: UDP client for data communication with the robot controller.

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.FileClientAccess
member.summary: Provides synchronized access to file transfer operations. File operations require exclusive access to prevent concurrent transfer corruption.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.FileClientAccess.FileClient
member.summary: UDP client dedicated to file transfer operations.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.FileClientAccess.#ctor(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase)
member.summary: Creates a new file access context with synchronized access to file operations.
member.param: The client instance to use for file operations.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.FileClientAccess.Dispose
member.summary: Releases the file client resources and synchronization lock.

member.name: F:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase._fileLockAccess
member.summary: Lock object for synchronizing file operations.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.IP
member.summary: Gets the IP address or hostname of the connected robot controller.

member.name: P:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.Connected
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the client is connected to a robot controller. Note: Since UDP is connectionless, this only indicates if the socket is configured.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.Close
member.summary: Closes the connection to the robot controller and releases network resources.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetAlarm(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotRecentAlarm)
member.summary: Reads alarm information from the robot controller. Retrieves alarm code, type, occurrence time, and descriptive text.
member.param: Which alarm to retrieve from the alarm history (latest, second latest, etc.).
member.returns: Alarm data containing code, type, time, and message text.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetStatusInformation
member.summary: Reads the current operational status of the robot controller. Returns information about mode (teach/play), running state, hold status, alarms, and servo power.
member.returns: Status data containing boolean flags for various robot states.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetExecutingJobInformation
member.summary: Reads information about the currently selected and executing job (program). Returns job name, current line, step, and speed override percentage.
member.returns: Job data containing name, line number, step, and speed override.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetJobStack(System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads the job call stack for a specific task on the robot controller. Returns the current nesting of CALL instructions, from the outermost job to the currently executing one.
member.param: Task number to query 0 for Master Job, 1 to 15 for sub-tasks. Default: 0 (Master Job).
member.returns: Job stack data containing the array of job names, outermost first.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetConfigurationInformation
member.summary: Reads axis configuration information for the default robot control group. Returns axis type names for each of the 8 possible axes.
member.returns: Axis configuration data with axis type names.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetConfigurationInformation(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotControlGroup)
member.summary: Reads axis configuration information for a specified control group. Returns axis type names (e.g., "S", "L", "U", "R", "B", "T") for each axis.
member.param: Control group to query (robot, base, station).
member.returns: Axis configuration data with axis type names.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetRobotCartesianPosition
member.summary: Reads the current robot Cartesian position (TCP position and orientation). Coordinates are returned in millimeters for X, Y, Z and degrees for Rx, Ry, Rz.
member.returns: Cartesian position data with coordinates in mm and degrees.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetRobotJointPosition
member.summary: Reads the current robot joint position in pulse (encoder) values. Returns raw pulse values for all robot axes.
member.returns: Joint position data with axis values in encoder pulses.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.DecodeRobotPositionData(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.PacketReader)
member.summary: Decodes robot position data from a packet reader response. Parses data type, posture form, tool/user coordinate numbers, and axis values.
member.param: Packet reader containing the position data.
member.returns: Decoded position data structure.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetRobotPosition(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotControlGroup)
member.summary: Reads position data for a specified control group. Can read robot, base, or station position data depending on the control group specified.
member.param: Control group to query.
member.returns: Position data with axis values (format depends on control group type).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetPositionError
member.summary: Reads the position error (difference between commanded and actual position) for the default robot. Values indicate servo tracking error in pulse units.
member.returns: Position error data with axis error values in pulses.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetPositionError(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotControlGroup)
member.summary: Reads the position error for a specified control group. Position error indicates the difference between commanded and actual position.
member.param: Control group to query.
member.returns: Position error data with axis error values in pulses.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetTorque
member.summary: Reads the current torque values for the default robot's servo motors. Torque values indicate motor load as a percentage of rated torque.
member.returns: Torque data with axis torque values (typically as percentage).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetTorque(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.RobotControlGroup)
member.summary: Reads the current torque values for a specified control group's servo motors. Torque values indicate motor load as a percentage of rated torque.
member.param: Control group to query.
member.returns: Torque data with axis torque values.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.AlarmReset(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.AlarmResetType)
member.summary: Resets alarms or cancels errors on the robot controller. Use Reset to clear alarm conditions after resolving the cause. Use Cancel for recoverable errors that don't require alarm reset.
member.param: Type of reset operation (Reset or Cancel).
member.returns: Response header indicating success.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.ServoCommand(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.OnOffCommandType,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Executes hold/servo/HLock on/off commands. For servo control, this enables or disables motor power to the robot. Servo must be ON for the robot to move.
member.param: Type of command (Hold, Servo, or HLock).
True to enable (ON), false to disable (OFF).
member.returns: Response header indicating success.
member.remarks: For servo ON commands, a longer timeout is used (PowerOnTimeoutMilliseconds) as brake release and motor initialization may take several seconds.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.SwitchingCommand(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.SwitchingCommands)
member.summary: Switches the robot execution mode between Step, Cycle, and Continuous.
member.param: Target execution mode.
member.returns: Response header indicating success.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.Display(System.String)
member.summary: Displays a popup message on the robot programming pendant. Message will appear as a notification to the operator.
member.param: Message text to display (maximum 24 characters).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.StartJob
member.summary: Starts execution of the currently selected job. The job must be selected first using SelectJob before calling this method. Servo must be ON and the robot must not be in alarm state.
member.returns: Response header indicating success.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.SelectJob(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Selects a job for execution and optionally positions to a specific line. Call StartJob after selecting to begin execution.
member.param: Job name (maximum 32 characters, typically ends with .JBI extension).
Line number within the job to position to (1-based). Use 0 for beginning.
member.returns: Response header indicating success.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetManagementTime(UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.ManagementTimeType,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves management time statistics from the robot controller. Can query various timing metrics like control power ON time, servo ON time, etc.

Index for job-specific timing (0 for main job, or job index).

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.ReadIO(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads multiple I/O bytes from the robot controller starting at a specified index.
member.param:
Starting I/O index. Valid ranges:
1-512: Robot user input signal
1001-1512: Robot user output signal
2001-2512: External input signal
2701-2956: Network input signal
3001-3512: External output signal
3701-3956: Network output signal
4001-4160: Robot system input signal
5001-5300: Robot system output signal
6001-6064: Interface panel input signal
7001-7999: Auxiliary relay signal
8001-8128: Robot control status signal
Number of bytes to read (will be rounded up to nearest even number).
member.returns: Plural data containing array of I/O byte values.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.WriteIO(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Byte})
member.summary: Writes I/O bytes to the robot controller starting at a specified index.
member.param: Starting I/O index. See ReadIO for valid ranges.
Data array to write (must contain an even number of elements).
member.returns: Response header indicating success.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetFile(System.String,UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.GetFileProgressDelegate)
member.summary: Downloads (saves) a file from the robot controller to the PC. Large files are received in multiple blocks and automatically reassembled. Special use case : to download CMOS.BIN, first perform a CMOS backup using BatchDataBackup, then use GetFile with the backup file path.
member.param: File name to download from the robot (e.g., "TEST.JBI").
Optional callback for progress notifications.
member.returns: File content data including the file name and text content.

member.name: M:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.HighSpeedEServer.Internal.HighSpeedEServerClientBase.BatchDataBackup(System.String)
member.summary: Performs a backup of the robot's CMOS. The CMOS.BIN file is copied locally in the robot controller to "/SPDRC/CMOSBK.BIN". The operation can take several seconds to complete. After this command, the backup file can be downloaded using GetFile("/SPDRC/CMOSBK.BIN"). To enable this command : in "MANAGEMENT MODE", select {SETUP} - {AUTO BACKUP SET} and set "DEVICE" to "RAMDISK". If this menu is not available, Reboot the controller in Maintenance Mode and set System / Setup / OPTION FUNCTION / AUTOBACKUP to "Used", then do a "Safety board flash reset" from File / Initialize
member.param: Controller backup file path for CMOS.BIN copy

member.name: T:UnderAutomation.Yaskawa.YaskawaRobot
member.summary: Main entry point for communicating with Yaskawa Motoman robots. This class provides methods to connect, monitor, and control the robot through multiple interfaces: High Speed Ethernet Server

remarks.para: The YaskawaRobot class encapsulates all communication functionality with Yaskawa Motoman robot controllers that support the High Speed Ethernet Server protocol This includes the DX200, YRC1000, YRC1000micro, and NX100 controllers.

Before using this class, ensure that:
The robot controller has the required communication function enabled
Network connectivity is established between your application and the robot
Appropriate safety measures are in place when controlling robot motion
